introSection: Steel Dragon is a steel spinning roller coaster located at Waldameer & Water World in Erie, Pennsylvania, United States. It was manufactured by Maurer AG and opened in 2004.

sections.title: History
sections.content: Steel Dragon opened to the public on July 2, 2004. It was in continuous operation since opening until 2025, when it remained closed at the beginning of the season due to the delayed installation of a new control panel. The ride reopened to the public on July 4th, 2025 following the successful installation of the new control system.

sections.title: Features
sections.content: The ride is a steel spinning coaster. The cars take a right turn out of the station followed by a lift hill. It falls down the largest drop at the top of the lift hill, as the cars begin to spin. The ride then hits an 80-degree bank. It completes one more rectangular circuit before returning to the station.

sections.title: Trains
sections.content: Steel Dragon has eight cars. The riders sit two in a row back-to-back in a car. Four riders can fit in a car at once. Waldameer typically runs a four-to-six car operation.

fullText:
Steel Dragon is a steel spinning roller coaster located at Waldameer & Water World in Erie, Pennsylvania, United States. It was manufactured by Maurer AG and opened in 2004.

Steel Dragon opened to the public on July 2, 2004. It was in continuous operation since opening until 2025, when it remained closed at the beginning of the season due to the delayed installation of a new control panel. The ride reopened to the public on July 4th, 2025 following the successful installation of the new control system.

The ride is a steel spinning coaster. The cars take a right turn out of the station followed by a lift hill. It falls down the largest drop at the top of the lift hill, as the cars begin to spin. The ride then hits an 80-degree bank. It completes one more rectangular circuit before returning to the station.

Steel Dragon has eight cars. The riders sit two in a row back-to-back in a car. Four riders can fit in a car at once. Waldameer typically runs a four-to-six car operation.
categories: Buildings and structures in Erie, Pennsylvania
